name: thomas-codex-workflows
summary: Personal Codex hooks for GitNexus upkeep, PR creation reminders, and worktree lifecycle support.
purpose: Use this plugin to make ordinary Codex sessions safer and more context-aware through hooks that run around shell commands, search commands, session start, and worktree create/remove events.
whenToUse: Keep GitNexus indexes fresh and inject graph context during code exploration.
Prepare and clean up worktree metadata during worktree lifecycle events.

safety.authModel: Uses local command state such as git, gh, and GitNexus without publishing credentials.
safety.writeModel: Hooks may block risky commands or update local worktree metadata, but do not publish remote changes by themselves.

installNotes: Hooks rely on the Codex compatibility variable for plugin root resolution.
The GitNexus hooks intentionally pass embeddings and skip AGENTS.md injection.
This plugin is machine-local behavior, not a source of secrets or project memory.
